data.title: Sensex, Nifty Hit Fresh Record High
data.description:
The BSE Sensex rose 227 points, or 0.4%, to 85,909, while the Nifty 50 climbed to 26,280, with both indices hitting record highs in Thursday morning trade.

The gains tracked a rally on Wall Street overnight, buoyed by bets on a Fed interest rate cut next month.

Expectations that the RBI may ease monetary policy at its December meeting further supported sentiment amid easing inflation and a robust GDP growth outlook.

Foreign capital inflows and falling oil prices also lifted sentiment, reinforcing expectations of softer inflation as India, the world’s third-largest oil importer, benefits from cheaper crude.

The broader market extended gains from the previous session, supported mainly by financial services, technology, and banking stocks.

Top performers included Bajaj Finance (2.2%), Bajaj Finserv (1.4%), HDFC Bank (1.2%), Larsen & Toubro (1.0%), and ICICI Bank (1.0%).

data.title: Singapore REITs to Accelerate Asset Buys, Sales, Enhancements — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore real-estate investment trusts are likely to accelerate asset acquisitions, divestments and enhancements as borrowing costs moderate, say analysts at Fitch Ratings. REITs' capitalization rates--a metric used to measure profitability--could compress amid rising demand, which could drive valuations higher, they say. They don't expect rated REITs to reduce debt significantly over the next 12-18 months, projecting that average Ebitda net leverage will decline to under 8.0X, compared with 8.1X in 2024. Mapletree Industrial Trust and Frasers Logistics & Commercial Trust have higher headroom than peers rated by Fitch and could deploy capital for portfolio rejuvenation and growth, the analysts add. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

South African Markets - Factors to watch on November 27
data.description:
The following scheduled economic events, equity and currency market moves may affect South African markets on Thursday.

ECONOMIC EVENTS

October producer price inflation (ZAPPIY=ECI)

SOUTH AFRICAN MARKETS

The South African rand strengthened on Wednesday after muted U.S. economic data bolstered expectations of an interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve in December, weighing on the dollar.

On the Johannesburg Stock Exchange, the Top-40 index was last up 1.3%.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian stocks rose on Thursday and the dollar was soft on growing expectations of an interest rate cut from the Federal Reserve next month, while the yen remained on intervention watch, with traders weighing the prospect of a rate hike before year-end.

WALL STREET

Wall Street extended its rally on Wednesday as revived tech strength and the increasing probability of a December interest rate cut from the U.S. Federal Reserve put investors in a buying mood the day before the Thanksgiving holiday.

GOLD

Gold edged lower on Thursday on profit-taking after it hit a near two-week high in the previous session, while investors weighed the possibility of a December U.S. interest rate cut amid conflicting signals from the Federal Reserve.

EMERGING MARKETS

NEWS24

data.title: Singapore Industrial, Logistics REITs' Growth in Rent-Renewal Rates Set to Hold Steady — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore logistics and industrial REITs are likely to see steady growth in rates of rents renewed next year, with Fitch Ratings putting gains at around 5%-10%. These REITs are likely to benefit from tailwinds in the growing e-commerce and advanced manufacturing sectors, driving demand for quality logistics and high-specification industrial space, the analysts say in a note. Still, they expect growth rates in renewed rents in 2026 to be more muted for Singapore industrial and logistics properties in Mapletree Industrial Trust, Frasers Logistics & Commercial Trust and ESR-REIT's portfolios, given a likely higher-than-average increase in new supply.(megan.cheah@wsj.com)

data.title: China, HK shares edge up on domestic AI optimism; Vanke triggers property sector slide
data.description:
China and Hong Kong stocks edged up on Thursday as chip makers and artificial intelligence-related sectors continued their rebound, while developer Vanke's debt woes triggered a property shares selloff that dragged on the market.

** At the midday break, the Shanghai Composite index was up 0.5% at 3,883.01, the blue-chip CSI300 index was up 0.3%.

** The CSI Semiconductor Index (.CSI931865) and the CSI AI Index both gained roughly 1%, extending recent gains.

** AI chip maker Cambricon Technologies rallied as much as 5.6% to a two-week high, while SMIC jumped as much as 3.6%.

** Optimism towards domestic AI sectors flared up again after The Information reported that the regulators have barred TikTok owner ByteDance from deploying Nvidia chips in new data centers.

** " China is going all-in to win its version of the AI race," analysts at Macquarie said in a note, adding that there could be more extended and accelerated infrastructure policy push supporting the sector.

** Dragging down the markets, shares of China Vanke tumbled as much as 8.8%, after the company said it was seeking to delay an onshore bond repayment for the first time.

** China's CSI 300 real estate index slid as much as 4.5% to the lowest since Sept 2024 on the Vanke woes, before closing down 1.5% at midday break.

** In Hong Kong, the benchmark Hang Seng Index climbed 0.3%, while the Hang Seng Mainland Property Index was flat after losing as much as 2%.

** On the data front, China's industrial profits fell in October after two months of growth, as businesses continued to grapple with lacklustre domestic demand and an export downturn.

** Elsewhere, Beijing announced a new plan to boost consumption in the world's second-largest economy, detailing measures that include promoting upgrades of consumer goods in rural areas and sectors such as pets and toys.

Indian automaker Mahindra launches electric SUV in EV push
data.description:
Indian automaker Mahindra & Mahindra launched a seven-seater electric SUV XEV 9S on Thursday with a starting price of around 2 million rupees ($22,409.97), pushing into a segment dominated by Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles .

"With the XEV 9S, we are not just playing in the EV segment, we're expanding it", Nalinikanth Gollagunta, CEO of the automotive division, said in a statement.

Bookings for the vehicle will open on January14, with deliveries starting on January 23, Mahindra, India's second-largest SUV manufacturer by volumes, said.

Tata's Harrier EVs are priced from around $23,500, and JSW MG Motor India's MG ZS EV starts near $15,000.

($1 = 89.2460 Indian rupees)

data.title: Key facts: HDFC Bank Drives Nifty Up 321 Points; Record Nifty Bank High
data.description:
• On November 26, 2025, HDFC Bank boosted the Nifty, which rose 321 points to 26,205, while the Nifty Bank index hit a record high of 59,528, up 708 points.1
  • On September 24, 2025, HDFC Bank executed multiple block deals, trading 292,136 shares at 1002.10 INR and 1,976,024 shares at 1007.75 INR, reflecting substantial trading activity.2

data.title: Key facts: HCL Technologies partners with SAP on Physical AI; shares rise 1.03%
data.description:
• HCL Technologies Ltd (HCLTECH) has entered into a collaboration with SAP to advance initiatives centered around Physical AI.1
  • HCL Technologies experienced a gain of 1.03 percent, reaching Rs 1,617.20, amid a broader rise in IT stocks influenced by expectations of a US Federal Reserve rate cut and a weakening Indian rupee.2

data.title: Key facts: Bajaj Finance shines in NBFC sector; shares rise 31% to 43%
data.description:
• Market expert Deven Choksey identified Bajaj Finance as a top performer in the NBFC sector, citing its rapid loan disbursement, effective capital management, and strong asset quality.1
  • Bajaj Finance closed up 1.24% at 26,205.30, contributing to the Nifty index's gains, with shares rising 31% to 43% since the peak on September 27, 2024.23

data.title: Key facts: Axis Bank issues Rs. 5,000 Crore debentures; Nifty rises 321 points; Anand Viswanathan named Chief Risk Officer
data.description:
• On November 26, 2025, Axis Bank issued 500,000 long-term debentures worth Rs. 5,000 Crore, with a 7.27% annual coupon rate, rated 'CRISIL AAA/Stable' and others.1
  • On November 26, 2025, Axis Bank boosted the Nifty, which rose by 321 points to 26,205, while the Nifty Bank index hit a record high of 59,528, up 708 points.2
  • Axis Bank Limited has appointed Anand Viswanathan as the new Chief Risk Officer, effective January 1, 2026, succeeding Amit Talgeri, who will conclude his second term on December 31, 2025.3

data.title: Key facts: Adani Enterprises rights issue dates set; IHC unit sells shares
data.description:
• Adani Enterprises Ltd (ADANIENT) is offering a rights issue. Key dates: rights entitlements credited on November 18, renunciation ends December 5, issue closes December 10, allotment on December 11.1
  • A unit of International Holding Company (IHC) executed a bulk deal, selling 842,924 shares of Adani Enterprises Ltd, as reported by the National Stock Exchange (NSE).2

data.title: Key facts: WIPRO LTD partners for AI research; faces performance challenges
data.description:
• WIPRO LTD has entered into a partnership to establish a joint research program focused on artificial intelligence (AI).1
  • Wipro Technologies has underperformed in the past year, facing challenges from AI disruption impacting routine jobs like call centers and admin support.2

data.title: Key facts: TCS to drive Aldi South's digital shift; partners with TPG on AI
data.description:
• Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) will lead digital transformation for Aldi South, enhancing its digital capabilities globally.1
  • Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) has entered into a partnership with private equity firm TPG, committing $2 billion in equity to establish AI data centres.2
  • On November 21, 2025, Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) shares rose 1.35% to Rs 3,162, driven by a potential US rate cut and a weaker rupee.34

data.title: Key facts: Infosys shares rise 1.93%; underperformance due to AI threats
data.description:
• Infosys shares rose 1.93% to Rs 1,559.55, boosting the Nifty IT index by 1.48%, driven by expectations of a US Federal Reserve rate cut and a weaker Indian rupee.1
  • Infosys has underperformed over the past year due to rising AI threats in the service sector, impacting routine jobs in call centers and admin support.2

data.title: Key facts: Indian Continent Investment sells 0.6% stake in Bharti Airtel; Strong ARPU growth expected; Bharti Telecom to raise $954 million
data.description:
• On November 26, 2025, Indian Continent Investment sold a 0.6% stake in Bharti Airtel, impacting the stock price, which closed at Rs 2,126.8 on the NSE.12
  • Bharti Airtel shows strong ARPU compared to Jio and improving operations in Africa. A potential ARPU of ₹300 is priced in, with a possible 15–20% increase aligned with market trends.3
  • Bharti Telecom plans to raise about $954 million by issuing short-term bonds with maturities of two and three years, offering annual coupons of 7.30% and 7.40%.4

data.title: Palm Oil Rises Further
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures rose for a second session on Thursday, hovering around MYR 4,060 per tonne amid strength in Dalian edible oils and extended bargain hunting.

Prices recovered further from a five-month low hit earlier this week, on expectations that India, the world’s largest buyer, may lift imports by about 20% in the new marketing year starting this November, supported by more competitive pricing.

In top supplier Indonesia, authorities have tightened oversight of the palm oil sector following suspected export data manipulation, a move that could slightly curb near-term shipments and provide limited price support.

So far this week, the contract is set for a second straight weekly decline, down around 0.2%, as supply-side pressures outweigh gains.

Cargo surveyors estimated Malaysia’s exports fell between 16.4%–18.8% in the first 25 days of November from the prior month, while October output surged 11% to its highest since August 2015, pushing inventories to a 6-1/2-year peak.

data.title: Train runs down, kills 11 in China's worst rail accident in over a decade
data.description:
A train ran into a group of railway workers on Thursday, killing 11 and injuring two, officials in China's southwestern city of Kunming said, the deadliest rail accident in more than a decade.

The train, testing earthquake detection equipment, hit workers on a curved section of track at the city's Luoyang Town railway station, the officials said.

The station in Yunnan province has resumed normal services, and the cause of the accident is being investigated, they added in a statement.

China's rail network is the world's largest, spanning more than 160,000 km (100,000 miles) and racking up billions of trips each year.

Though praised for its efficiency, it has drawn scrutiny after several high-profile incidents, such as a 2011 crash in the eastern province of Zhejiang that killed 40 and injured 200.

Nine were killed in 2021 when a train in the northwestern province of Gansu ran into workers on a section of the Lanzhou-Xinjiang railway.
